title: DAVID — The Language No One Wrote Down

notes: Row 6: AI-disclosure obligation planned — verify presence at Pre-Publish.
Social = mass dissemination. Platform AI-label + community guidelines apply.
Row 1: STUDIO Casting Bible synthetic cast — Upon Tyne ownership; no third-party likeness license.
Gate 0 cleared for Development/Pre-Production subject to ongoing review.

config.voice_suffix: mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Archive-001: identical Archive interior — floor-to-ceiling manuscript shelves, long wooden worktable, open illuminated codex, cuneiform tablet, rolled papyrus, single brass desk lamp, same shelf blur depth, same eye-line to desk — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host seated at Archive worktable under neutral 5000K ambient key, speaks calmly to camera; motivated desk practical visible on codex surface only. Camera: slow gentle push-in medium. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Motivated desk practical in lower desk quadrant only — zero warm spill on host face, sweater, or shadows. Forbid yellow-green cast. Forbid blue-starved generation. Forbid magenta cast. Forbid global orange skin push. Motivated practical accent only — no unmotivated beauty dish, no fashion rim, lamp or window must be visible or implied. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "I am DAVID. Today, Proto-Indo-European — the ancestor of Sanskrit, Latin, Greek, English, Persian, and hundreds more — a language spoken around five thousand years ago that no one ever wrote down." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Opening shot, establish environment slowly.
shots.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Proto-Indo-European — the ancestor of Sanskrit, Latin, Greek, English, Persian, and hundreds more — a language spoken around five thousand years ago that no one ever wrote down.

Lip-synced, delivers: "In 1786, William Jones stood before the Asiatic Society of Bengal and announced that Sanskrit bore to Greek and Latin a stronger affinity than could possibly have been produced by accident — and that all three likely sprang from a common source. Within a century, Rask, Bopp, and Grimm had laid the foundations of comparative linguistics, and the ancestor language — never written, never recorded — began to take shape through method alone." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: In 1786, William Jones stood before the Asiatic Society of Bengal and announced that Sanskrit bore to Greek and Latin a stronger affinity than could possibly have been produced by accident — and that all three likely sprang from a common source. Within a century, Rask, Bopp, and Grimm had laid the foundations of comparative linguistics, and the ancestor language — never written, never recorded — began to take shape through method alone.

Lip-synced, delivers: "We reconstruct Proto-Indo-European corpus-first — but the corpus is the daughter languages themselves: Grimm's Law governs Germanic consonant shifts, Brugmann's Law governs vowel lengthening, and Hittite laryngeals confirmed what Saussure predicted from internal reconstruction alone." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.

Host settles back slightly, reflective scholarly mode — hands composed on desk, slight lean back. Camera: medium — slight pullback. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "In 1868, August Schleicher composed the first sentence in Proto-Indo-European — a short fable about a sheep and horses — to demonstrate the reconstruction in practice. Scholars have revised it many times since as our methods improved. The dominant view today places the original speakers on the Pontic-Caspian steppe, with migration carrying the language westward into Europe and eastward into South Asia — though the Anatolian hypothesis, proposing an earlier origin in Anatolia, remains a serious alternative." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: In 1868, August Schleicher composed the first sentence in Proto-Indo-European — a short fable about a sheep and horses — to demonstrate the reconstruction in practice. Scholars have revised it many times since as our methods improved. The dominant view today places the original speakers on the Pontic-Caspian steppe, with migration carrying the language westward into Europe and eastward into South Asia — though the Anatolian hypothesis, proposing an earlier origin in Anatolia, remains a serious alternative.

provenance_card.title: DAVID · The Archive

provenance_card.footer: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.
